scenario: Catalyst Insurance configures user tasks in Tasklist for claims agents across 5000 claims per day in Camunda 8.8. The team wants the task tile to display a short descriptive line so reviewers can distinguish similar approvals at a glance, and wants to know which reserved variable controls this display on the tile.
question: Which reserved process variable name controls the descriptive line shown on a task tile in Camunda 8.8 Tasklist?

options.text: A reserved variable named taskTileSubtitle that is parsed by Tasklist and rendered as the secondary descriptive line on the task tile for the assigned user
A reserved variable named camundaTaskDescription that is read from the user task element documentation and rendered as the descriptive line on the tile
A reserved variable named taskContextDisplayName whose value is rendered as the descriptive line on the task tile in Tasklist for the assigned user
A reserved variable named userTaskHeaderLabel that is set as a custom header on the activated job and rendered as the descriptive task line on the tile

a.text: Incorrect. Tasklist does not read a reserved variable named taskTileSubtitle for the descriptive line. The reserved variable for the descriptive line on a task tile is taskContextDisplayName according to the variables concept page in the documentation.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no reserved variable named camundaTaskDescription in Camunda 8.8, and the task documentation field is not rendered as the descriptive line on the task tile in Tasklist for assigned users.

c.text: Correct. The Context variable section identifies taskContextDisplayName as the reserved variable whose value is shown on the task tile, and notes that the name is reserved exclusively for this purpose and should not be reused elsewhere.

d.text: Incorrect. Custom headers on a user task are not rendered as the task tile label. The reserved variable taskContextDisplayName carries the descriptive line shown on the tile according to the variables concept page.

explanation: Context variables describe the context of a task and group variables into a descriptive label. The reserved variable name taskContextDisplayName is used exclusively for this purpose, and its value is rendered on the task tile in Tasklist. Context variables are not currently available in Tasklist V2 according to the migration notes.
